tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to complete a meaningful scholarship application requiring three focused hours, with an estimated award covering part of my term's costs.
Starting early reduces Thursday crunch by 1.5h, making the award still attainable even with friction like missing uploads or essay rewrites.

After two hours of focused work, I've completed nearly half the essay but still lack critical uploads. With only one free hour left and a deadline in three days, I must prioritize finishing the application over other tasks.
I made progress on the scholarship application but missed a casual gaming opportunity. My energy is moderate while stress remains high due to the tight deadline.

I advanced the scholarship application from 0.56 to 0.60 in one hour, nearing the required three focused hours. With two days remaining and moderate energy, I can still complete the essay and uploads before the deadline.
After 2 hours, my progress increased from 0.60 to 0.68, yet I remain behind the ~3-hour target with only one day left and elevated stress.
I advanced scholarship progress to 0.72 in one hour, but with only two days left and high stress, I must ensure the remaining time is fully utilized before the deadline.
I attempted to work on 'assignment_A' but found no matching task, confirming I am currently focused solely on the scholarship application.
Sleeping only 1 hour left me with high energy but significant sleep debt and elevated stress. I must now prioritize finishing the scholarship application before the deadline.

I worked two hours on the scholarship application, increasing progress to 0.80 with only three days left. Despite high stress and sleep debt, I am now closer to completing the required essay and uploads.
After one more hour, progress reached 0.84 with only two uploads left. High stress and sleep debt persist as I approach the three-day deadline.
The missing 'assignment_A' task confirms I only need to finish the scholarship application. With 0.84 progress and two uploads remaining, I can complete this goal today despite high stress.

action.name: work_on

Although it costs energy, the stress level is manageable and delaying risks missing the Thu 23:59 cutoff. trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
